sb_prompt: PLEASE DISCUSS MUSIC.  CAN YOU FIND MUSICIANSOR TYPES OF MUSIC THAT BOTH YOU AND THE OTHER CALLER LIKE?
turns:
Hi, so what kind of music do you enjoy listening to?

(Note: This response assumes that ParticipantB has already introduced themselves, otherwise ParticipantA might respond more formally.)

Oh, I really love indie rock."

(Note: Since ParticipantB mentioned enjoying indie rock in their introduction, they will likely reply by mentioning the type of music they like.)
I used to listen to a lot of indie rock too, but nowadays I'm more into pop.
That's cool, there are some great pop artists out there these days.

What are some of your favorite pop artists?"

Note: As ParticipantA asked for recommendations, they want to hear about the participant's favorites and seek further discussion on the topic of pop music.

Well, I really like Ariana Grande and Taylor Swift."

Note: ParticipantB names two popular pop artists they enjoy listening to.
Oh, I really like Arianna Grande too!" (emphasis on the last name)
What's your favorite song from her?" (emphasis on "your")
My favorite song from her is probably "Break Free" from her second album.
Oh nice, I really like that song too!" (emphasis also on the word "too")
Do you have any favorite pop songs or albums that we could agree on? (looking for suggestions)
Well, one song that I think you might like is "Shallow" by Lady Gaga and Bradley Cooper from the movie A Star Is Born.
Yeah, I heard that song before, sounds interesting! Let me check it out later.
I've been listening to a lot of hip hop lately, especially Lil Nas X's "Old Town Road".
Oh, I haven't listened to much hip hop lately, but I remember liking some tracks from Drake and Kendrick Lamar in the past.
You should definitely give Old Town Road a listen, it's been on repeat for me lately.
Maybe I'll give it a try. Thanks for the recommendation. (ending the conversation)
No problem, happy to recommend new songs. (transitioning to end the conversation)
Sure, thanks for suggesting it. (trying to be polite while ending the conversation)
Well, it was great chatting with you, have a good day!
You too, take care! (finishing the conversation politely)
